sector: Semiconductors — magnetic sensing (Hall/TMR) + power ICs (isolated SiC/GaN gate drivers, current sensors); end-markets automotive (xEV/ADAS), industrial, and a fast-ramping AI data-center power/sensing vertical.
bull_thesis: ALGM is a high-quality, IP-rich sensing franchise that has grafted a genuine AI-capex optionality leg onto a recovering content-growth core. FY2026: revenue $890M (+23% YoY), Q4 $243M (+26% YoY); non-GAAP GM 49.4% FY / 50.0% Q4 (+140 bps YoY DESPITE a ~200 bps gold-cost headwind); op margin 14.1% FY / 15.6% Q4; non-GAAP EPS $0.54 (>2x YoY); record FCF $125M; $60M voluntary debt paydown; net debt only ~$116M (<0.7x EBITDA); term loan repriced -25 bps to SOFR+175. The new leg is the prize: data center quadrupled to ~10% of FY sales, 14% of Q4 (+41% q/q), guided to 16-17% of Q1'27 sales (20-25% sequential). Within DC, current sensors went from 'virtually 0 entering FY23' to ~20% of DC revenue by Q4 — Allegro is leveraging its CORE magnetic-sensing IP (the real moat) into 800V HVDC AI racks, not fighting commodity power switches head-on. Content-per-rack scales '$150 today to over $425 in next-gen AI configurations.' Legacy engine is also strengthening: total auto +17% (well above SAAR+7-10%), Focused Auto (xEV/ADAS) +30% to $349M, industrial +38%; TMR leadership (industry-first 10MHz TMR current sensor, EDN 2025 Sensor Product of the Year, ~30% of new releases) and isolated SiC gate drivers each carry a '2-3x content uplift' as customers move to 800V xEV/steer-by-wire. Design wins +30% YoY, backlog at a multiyear high. Two Chinese humanoid-robotics design wins seed the NEXT optionality leg. If DC converts and auto restocks, ALGM compounds revenue mid-teens-plus with GM climbing toward 52%.
bear_thesis: The business is excellent; the PRICE has already paid for the optionality. ALGM trades ~$59, ~$11B market cap, fwd P/E ~56x, EV/Sales ~12.5x, EV/EBITDA >100x trailing — it 'soared' alongside MPWR in June 2026. That multiple underwrites a TRIPLE: data-center content converts at scale AND the auto cycle re-accelerates AND margins keep climbing. Three problems. (1) The DC leg is ONE fiscal year old, lumpy, and hyperscaler-capex-dependent; a digestion pause hits the highest-multiple part of the story first. (2) ALGM is NOT a named anchor on NVIDIA's published 800-VDC ecosystem partner roster (Infineon, Navitas, ADI, Renesas, STMicro, TI are the named partners) — it ships components INTO the architecture and competes in the more contested power layer against bigger, deeper-pocketed rivals (ON, Infineon, TI). (3) Management itself flagged 'somewhat thin inventory in automotive' with 'no clear signs of restocking at a broad level' — 71% of revenue is recovering off a trough, not booming, so an auto air-pocket would undercut the base while the DC multiple is extended. The thesis is not resolution-independent: the marginal growth AND the multiple both hinge on AI-capex durability — the exact shared macro factor saturating the rest of Basket D. There is little margin of safety; this is a quality name at a price where the asymmetry is to the downside.
sop_bull: $72-78/share. Whole-company intrinsic on a forward-earnings/EV-Sales basis with a DC content overlay. Core auto+industrial ($762M of FY26 rev, growing ~15-18% with GM rising to ~50%) valued at ~9-10x EV/Sales given the sensing-IP moat and content compounding = ~$7.0-7.6B EV. Data-center leg (run-rating ~$135M+ exiting FY26, guided to 16-17% of a growing top line, +40%+ growth, mapped to 800V HVDC) valued at a premium ~18-22x EV/Sales as a hyperscaler-power optionality = ~$2.6-3.2B EV. Robotics/humanoid optionality + buyback/deleverage credit ~$0.4-0.6B. Sum EV ~$10.0-11.4B, less ~$116M net debt, /186M shares = ~$53-61 base, with the bull DC ramp pushing the blended fair value to ~$72-78 if FY27 DC reaches ~20% of sales at a sustained premium multiple and auto restocks. Equivalent to ~50-55x a FY28 EPS power of ~$1.40-1.50.
sop_bear: $38-44/share. Re-rate to a quality-cyclical-analog multiple once AI-capex optionality is discounted. Whole company on ~22-26x a normalized FY27 non-GAAP EPS of ~$0.85-1.00 (auto soft, no restock, DC growth decelerates as hyperscalers digest) = ~$40 equity value, /186M shares net of net debt. Cross-check on EV/Sales: ~6-7x ~$1.0B FY27 sales = ~$6.5-7.0B EV less $116M net debt /186M = ~$34-37; blend ~$38-44. This is roughly where ALGM would settle if it re-rated toward ON (~32-38x fwd) / NXP (~19x fwd) rather than the MPWR (~50x fwd) nosebleed cohort — i.e., a ~30-35% drawdown from ~$59.
sop_breakdown: Base case blends a high-quality sensing-IP core with a discounted (not zeroed) DC optionality. Core auto+industrial: ~8.5x EV/Sales on ~$770M FY27 core sales = ~$6.5B EV. Data center: ~16x EV/Sales on ~$160M FY27 DC sales = ~$2.6B EV (premium for 800V/AI exposure, haircut for one-year history + non-anchor status on NVIDIA's roster + capex beta). Robotics/other optionality + net-cash-trajectory credit: ~$0.4B. Total EV ~$9.5-10.5B, less ~$116M net debt, /186M shares = ~$50-56; round to a $52-58 fair-value band, roughly in line with the ~$59 quote. Implies ~36-40x a FY28 EPS power near ~$1.40 — premium to ON/NXP, discount to MPWR, appropriate for the quality + the unproven durability of the DC leg.
risk_reward: Poor-to-balanced. At ~$59: upside-to-fair-value ~ -5% to -2% (price already at/above the $52-58 base band); upside-to-bull ~$72-78 = +22% to +32% (requires the full DC-converts + auto-restocks + margin-climb triple); downside-to-bear ~$38-44 = -25% to -36% on an AI-capex-digestion or auto air-pocket re-rate. Skew is roughly 1.0x up vs 1.3x down — asymmetry is to the DOWNSIDE because the optionality is in the price and the thesis is not resolution-independent.
catalyst_status: PENDING_HARD (with a partly-FIRED component). The load-bearing catalyst is the data-center capacity ramp: DC went 10% FY26 -> 14% Q4 (+41% q/q) -> GUIDED 16-17% of Q1 FY2027 sales (20-25% sequential) per the Q4 FY2026 release (FY ended Mar 27, 2026). That ramp is real, dated, and still inflecting forward (PENDING_HARD). BUT the AI-capex re-rate is partly already FIRED — ALGM 'soared' alongside MPWR in June 2026 and the multiple (fwd P/E ~56x, EV/Sales ~12.5x) already discounts the optionality. Verification gap: ALGM is NOT named on NVIDIA's published 800-VDC ecosystem partner roster (Infineon, Navitas, ADI, Renesas, STMicro, TI are named, per Fierce Sensors, 2026); ALGM ships current sensors (ACS37017/ACS37200) and Power-Thru SiC gate drivers INTO the 800V architecture (allegromicro.com / GlobeNewswire, Jan 2026) but no singular hyperscaler design-win award is publicly confirmed. So: real ramp, no anchor-award catalyst, and the re-rate is largely spent.
peer_comps_note: TRUE peers within the scale-out PWR-SEMI / sensing layer (live multiples verified Jun 2026): MONOLITHIC POWER (MPWR) — the closest valuation comp and the AI-power darling: ~$1,566/share, fwd P/E ~50x, NTM EV/EBITDA ~49x (vs ~21x peer median); ALGM sits in the same nosebleed cohort (fwd P/E ~56x) — i.e., ALGM is priced WITH the most expensive name in the group. onsemi (ON) — direct power/SiC competitor for 800V: fwd P/E ~32-38x, EV/EBITDA ~15x; cheaper, more cyclical, larger. NXP (NXPI) — auto/industrial analog peer: fwd P/E ~19x, EV/EBITDA ~17x — the value anchor of the cohort. Infineon, TI, ADI, Renesas, STMicro — the NAMED NVIDIA 800-VDC partners and ALGM's true power-layer competitors (broader, deeper). RANKING: ALGM is TOP-DECILE on VALUATION RICHNESS (alongside MPWR, ~2.5-3x NXP's multiple); STRONG on GROWTH (+23% FY26, DC quadrupling); GOOD/recovering on MARGINS (GM ~50%, op ~14-16% — below MPWR's ~58% GM but improving fast); MID on RETURNS (ROIC normalizing off a trough, not durably above peers); and HIGH but NON-ANCHOR on AI-capex EXPOSURE (real DC ramp, but ships-into rather than a named NVIDIA partner). Net: ALGM is priced like MPWR but with onsemi-class competitive exposure in its fastest-growing layer.
role_in_scaleout: ALGM supplies the precision current-sensing (Hall/TMR) and isolated SiC gate-driver silicon that meters and switches power inside 800V-DC AI racks and the upstream power-conversion chain — a real but non-exclusive picks-and-shovels role in the electrical-distribution/800V-power-semi bottleneck; demand is durable as a build-out beneficiary (electrification + AI power) but cyclically capex-beta and contested by larger power-semi incumbents, so the role is criticality-MEDIUM, durability-GOOD-not-essential.
consensus_delta: The street is NOT looking the wrong way here — it is looking RIGHT at the data-center story and has bid the stock accordingly (fwd P/E ~56x, EV/Sales ~12.5x; 'soared' alongside MPWR in June 2026). There is no hidden mispricing in our favor; if anything the consensus error is in the OTHER direction — the multiple now assumes (1) the one-year-old DC leg converts the '$150->$425 per rack' content opportunity at scale, (2) auto restocks even though management explicitly said 'no clear signs of restocking at a broad level,' and (3) GM keeps climbing toward 52%. That is a triple priced as a near-certainty. The most factually-exposed street assumption is that ALGM is an anchor beneficiary of NVIDIA's 800-VDC architecture — NVIDIA's published partner roster names Infineon, Navitas, ADI, Renesas, STMicro and TI, NOT Allegro; ALGM ships components into the architecture and competes in the contested power layer. The expectations arbitrage is unfavorable: little daylight between price and even the bull SoP.
valley_of_death: No balance-sheet cliff — net debt is only ~$116M (<0.7x EBITDA), FCF is a record $125M, and the term loan was just repriced lower; solvency is a non-issue. The near-term (3-9 month) risk is a MULTIPLE event, not a cash event: (1) any hyperscaler-capex digestion headline or an AI-capex 'guide-down' (the regime doc flags this as the dominant ~40%-of-2026-EPS-growth shared factor) would de-rate the highest-multiple DC leg first; (2) the auto end-market is recovering off a trough with thin inventory and no broad restock — an auto air-pocket undercuts 71% of revenue while the DC multiple is extended; (3) the gold-cost headwind and the climb toward a >52% GM leave little room for a margin miss against a 50-51% guide. The most likely 'valley' is simply mean-reversion of a stock that has already run, on the first DC-growth-deceleration print or auto disappointment.
positioning_washout: No yield/passive base to flush — ALGM pays no dividend; the holder base is growth/AI-momentum (institutional ownership ~76%), the cohort that BOUGHT the June 2026 melt-up. That cuts the wrong way for a long entry: there is no forced-seller capitulation to buy into; instead the risk is a momentum-unwind / de-grossing if AI-capex sentiment cracks (the regime doc's 'AI-capex break' tripwire and stretched-positioning warning — BofA FMS cash 3.9%, little dry powder). The washout to wait for would be an AI-de-grossing flush that resets the multiple, not a structural shareholder rotation.
forcing_function: The next hard dates are the quarterly DC-mix prints: Q1 FY2027 results (~late-July/early-Aug 2026) which must validate the guided 16-17% DC mix and 20-25% sequential DC growth, then Q2 FY2027. A clean beat that confirms DC durability extends the premium; any sequential DC deceleration or an auto-restock miss is the event that forces a re-rate toward the ON/NXP cohort. There is NO singular binding catalyst (no confirmed hyperscaler award, no deal, no proxy event) — the re-rate driver is recurring earnings prints, which is exactly why the asymmetry is thin: the good news is already in the tape and each print is a fresh chance to disappoint.
moderator_conclusion: PASS (C), conviction 2. ALGM is a genuinely high-quality, well-run franchise — accelerating revenue (+23% FY26), margins expanding against a real gold-cost headwind, record FCF, active deleveraging, a defensible magnetic-sensing IP core (TMR leadership), and a real, fast-ramping AI-data-center leg (DC 10%->14% Q4->16-17% guided) that leverages that core IP into 800V HVDC racks. Forensic quality is high (Interrogator 4/5, STRENGTHENING, credible/candid management). But this is a regime-and-valuation PASS, not a quality PASS. Applying the regime doc: the favorable window rewards HARD-DATED, resolution-INDEPENDENT catalysts and PENALIZES already-run AI-capex names whose thesis hinges on the single shared macro factor saturating the basket. ALGM is exactly that name — it 'soared' alongside MPWR in June 2026 to fwd P/E ~56x / EV/Sales ~12.5x, it is NOT a named NVIDIA 800-VDC anchor partner (Infineon/Navitas/ADI/Renesas/STMicro/TI are), and its DC leg is one fiscal year old and capex-beta. The base-case SoP (~$52-58) sits at/below the ~$59 price; risk/reward skews to the downside (~+25-30% bull vs ~-25-35% bear) and the catalyst is PENDING_HARD-but-largely-FIRED (the re-rate is spent). value_conviction is also 2: even stripping the AI overlay, you are paying a premium-to-fair-value multiple for a NARROW-moat, cyclically-recovering business — there is no margin of safety, so the value case is no stronger than the catalyst case. WATCHLIST behavior, not entry: revisit on an AI-capex de-grossing flush that resets the multiple toward the ON/NXP cohort, or a print that confirms DC durability AND a cheaper entry. As-is, the optionality is in the price and the thesis is not independent of the rest of Basket D.
interrogator_dossier:
# Interrogator Forensic Dossier — Allegro MicroSystems (ALGM)

Prepared as of 2026-06-22. Source: Q4/FY2026 earnings release & call (fiscal year ended March 27, 2026), Q1-Q4 FY2026 commentary, and live fundamentals/valuation. Transcript source: web. ALGM is a normally-listed Nasdaq equity (ticker ALGM).

## 1. NARRATIVE ARC
Allegro entered FY2026 filed under 'automotive/industrial magnetic-sensor and power-IC supplier levered to xEV content' — a SAAR-plus content-growth story. Across FY2026 the story acquired a SECOND engine: the data-center vertical went from a rounding error to the headline. Framing migrated from 'we beat SAAR by 7-10 points on content' to a dual-narrative: (1) the durable auto/industrial content compounder, plus (2) an emergent, fast-ramping AI-data-center power/sensing franchise. Data center quadrupled in FY2026 to ~10% of total sales, hitting 14% of Q4 sales (+41% sequential), with Q1 FY2027 guided to 16-17% of sales (20-25% sequential). Within data center, current sensors went from 'virtually 0 entering FY23' to ~20% of DC revenue by Q4 — mapping directly onto NVIDIA's 800-VDC architecture roadmap. Load-bearing content claim: '$150 per rack today -> over $425 in next-generation AI configurations.' The legacy engine strengthened too: total auto +17%, Focused Auto (xEV/ADAS) +30% to $349M, industrial +38%; TMR sensing (~30% of releases, industry-first 10MHz TMR current sensor) and isolated SiC gate drivers each carry a '2-3x content uplift' claim. Genuine expansion, not substitution.

## 2. CLAIMS vs FINANCIALS (forensic)
Growth re-accelerating: FY26 rev $890M (+23%); Q4 $243M (+26% YoY, +6% q/q); Q1'27 guide $245-255M — CORROBORATED. Margins recovering: Q4 non-GAAP GM 50.0% (vs 45.6% PY-Q4); FY26 GM 49.4% (+140 bps YoY DESPITE ~200 bps gold headwind); Q1'27 guide 50-51% — CORROBORATED and impressive. Operating leverage: FY26 op margin 14.1%, Q4 15.6%; non-GAAP EPS $0.54 FY (>2x YoY), Q4 $0.17 (+183% YoY) — CORROBORATED. Cash/deleverage: FY26 FCF record $125M; $60M voluntary debt repayment; year-end net debt $116M; term loan repriced -25 bps to SOFR+175 — CORROBORATED, clean balance sheet. Backlog multiyear-high / design wins +30%: plausible, partly unverifiable (management-defined). DC durable new leg: ramp CORROBORATED, durability UNPROVEN (one fiscal year, lumpy, capex-dependent). The narrative is RICHER than the trajectory on the '$150->$425' per-rack content (opportunity framing, not booked revenue). HONEST: management flagged 'somewhat thin inventory in automotive' with 'no clear signs of restocking at a broad level' — a credibility green flag. Note: aggregators showing ~$725M TTM rev / GAAP losses are the FY2025 trough lagging; the FY2026 release is the correct basis.

## 3. TONE & CONFIDENCE TRAJECTORY
Rising — and earned by the print. Early-year language defended content-over-SAAR against a soft auto tape; by Q4 the CEO leads with data-center records and 'multiyear-high backlog' and the CFO quantifies the current-sensor ramp. Shift from defensive (defend content through a destock) to offensive (new TAM, content-per-rack, robotics wins) — but paired with a defensive auto-restocking admission. Confidence without bravado. No tonal red flags.

## 4. GUIDANCE CREDIBILITY
Medium-High. Delivered into a sequentially improving guide and beat into Q4. Margin trajectory hit precisely despite the gold headwind. Q1'27 guide internally consistent, not obviously sandbagged. Caveat: a rising share of the guide leans on DC sequential growth (one year of history, high capex beta) — reliability high, durability less tested.

## 5. ANALYST PRESSURE POINTS
DC durability / customer concentration (how much is one or two hyperscaler programs; design-win-to-revenue conversion; is '$425/rack' contracted or aspirational; light named-customer disclosure). Auto restocking timing (management's own 'no clear signs of restocking'). Gold/input-cost pass-through and the GM ceiling. China/robotics optionality (two humanoid wins, unsized).

## 6. RED FLAGS / GREEN FLAGS
GREEN: margin expansion against a real cost headwind; honest auto-restock caveat; record FCF + voluntary deleveraging + loan reprice; genuinely new TAM mapped to NVIDIA 800-VDC; TMR technology leadership. RED: valuation has fully discounted the optionality (fwd P/E ~56x, EV/Sales ~12.5x, EV/EBITDA >100x; 'soared' with MPWR June 2026); single-factor dependence on AI-capex durability; DC revenue one year old and lumpy; auto end-market still soft (71% of revenue recovering off a trough).

## 7. HIDDEN SIGNALS
CFO's unprompted current-sensor quantification signals the DC story is a current-SENSING story (leveraging the core IP moat), more defensible than commodity power switches. 'Opex down sequentially' alongside +23% revenue = quiet operating-leverage signal. Robotics/humanoid wins seed the NEXT optionality leg before DC is mature. Persistent 'SAAR +7-10%' anchor = management still wants to be valued on content, a tell the auto tape is soft.

## 8. CAPITAL-ALLOCATION VERDICT
Trajectory STRENGTHENING. Accelerating revenue, expanding margins against a cost headwind, record FCF, active deleveraging, a genuinely new fast-ramping TAM on a durable sensing-IP core. The single thing the director must weigh: the quality is real but the PRICE has already paid for the optionality — at ~56x forward / ~12.5x EV/Sales, ALGM is priced for a triple (DC converts + auto re-accelerates + margins climb), the marginal buyer is underwriting AI-capex durability (the shared basket factor), and there is little margin of safety. MOAT: NARROW — genuine intangible/IP + design-in switching-cost in magnetic sensing (Hall/TMR), but contested in raw power switching vs ON/Infineon/TI, with returns cyclically recovering not durably high. MOAT_TREND STABLE (sensing IP firm; the fastest-growing dollars are in the more contested power layer). SECULAR_THREAT none — ALGM is a beneficiary of electrification + AI-power build-out; the risk is cyclical (AI-capex digestion + auto cycle), not terminal.

CREDIBILITY_SCORE: 4 | TRAJECTORY: STRENGTHENING | MOAT: NARROW | MOAT_TREND: STABLE | SECULAR_THREAT: none
skeptic_corrections: (1) The bear's load-bearing 'NVIDIA non-anchor' claim VERIFIES even harder than stated: NVIDIA's own developer blog (the 800-VDC architecture announcement) names its silicon providers as Analog Devices, Infineon, Innoscience, MPS, Navitas, OnSemi, Renesas, ROHM, STMicroelectronics and Texas Instruments — Allegro appears NOWHERE on NVIDIA's published roster. ALGM ships components INTO the architecture but is not a named ecosystem partner; the picks-and-shovels role is real but non-exclusive and contested by larger incumbents on the list. (2) ANCHOR-SOURCE CAVEAT: the '16-17% of Q1 FY2027 sales / 20-25% sequential' DC-mix guide is NOT in the filed 8-K / press release — the 8-K confirms only the Q4 '14% of total sales' DC figure and the $245-255M / $0.19-0.23 Q1'27 guide. The 16-17% DC-mix number is earnings-CALL-sourced, not a filed figure; treat it as management color, not a hard filed guide. (3) DELEVERAGE WORDING: the 8-K shows a $285M term-loan refinance and $345M of term-loan repayments during FY26 (cash $168.8M vs LT debt $285.7M = net debt ~$117M, matching the ~$116M claim) — the '$60M voluntary paydown' framing is a net of those flows, but the net-debt / clean-balance-sheet conclusion holds. None of these break the thesis; they reinforce the bear (non-anchor confirmed) and tighten one over-precise catalyst figure.
skeptic_evidence: NVIDIA Developer Blog, 'NVIDIA 800 VDC Architecture Will Power the Next Generation of AI Factories' (2026) — silicon providers named: Analog Devices, Infineon, Innoscience, MPS, Navitas, OnSemi, Renesas, ROHM, STMicroelectronics, Texas Instruments; Allegro NOT named.

Allegro MicroSystems FY2026 8-K / press release, GlobeNewswire 2026-05-07 (FY ended Mar 27, 2026): FY net sales $890.1M (+23%), Q4 $243.2M (+26%), non-GAAP GM 49.4% FY / 50.0% Q4, non-GAAP EPS $0.54 FY / $0.17 Q4, FCF $124.9M; Data Center 'record 14% of total Q4 sales'.

Allegro FY2026 8-K (SEC EDGAR, algm-ex99_1, filed 2026-05-07): cash & equivalents $168.8M vs long-term debt $285.7M as of Mar 27, 2026 (net debt ~$117M); Q1 FY2027 guide $245-255M net sales / $0.19-0.23 non-GAAP EPS — the press release contains NO 16-17% DC-mix guidance figure.
Navitas/NVIDIA MGX 800-VDC partner ceremony, GlobeNewswire 2026-06-03 — names the NVIDIA MGX 800-VDC ecosystem partners; Allegro absent, corroborating non-anchor status.

scale.role: Magnetic sensing + power-conversion; DC a record 14% of Q4 sales, ramping.
scale.rationale: Real DC-converts ramp (record 14% of Q4 sales) but the larger base is cyclical auto-sensing, and ALGM is NOT a named NVIDIA 800V ecosystem partner (it ships components in, non-exclusively) — a second-order TACTICAL franchise. Skeptic verdict CONFIRMED_WITH_CORRECTIONS, and the corrections REINFORCE the bear (non-anchor confirmed; the 16-17% DC-mix guide is call color, not a filed figure), so a ~8pt haircut off a ~44 franchise. The AI re-rate partly fired; second-order, wait.
scale.demand_durability: Medium — DC ramp is real but auto-sensing is the larger, more cyclical base.
